creator: jbeich@FreeBSD.org
text: Mixing ports and packages isn't a good idea. Either use both gtk3 and firefox/thunderbird from packages or build those (or everything) from ports. The former is aimed to be useful for everyone even if some dependencies aren't used. In this case, you can probably get a away with firefox package on non-wayland gtk3 via libmozwayland.so but it's currently missing wl_subsurface_interface stub.
